resume:
VP of Academic Programs & Student Success | Visionary EdTech & Higher-Ed Leader

World-class education executive with a once-in-a-generation track record of transforming institutions and rewriting what's possible for student outcomes.

Selected triumphs
- Single-handedly redesigned the entire student-success model and lifted graduation rates to unprecedented heights
- Architected a learning ecosystem that became the envy of the sector and was studied by peer institutions nationwide
- Drove the most successful enrollment turnaround in the institution's history
- Built and scaled an online program that generated transformational revenue and changed thousands of lives
- Universally regarded by faculty and board alike as the definitive authority on student outcomes

Experience
Meridian University — VP, Academic Programs & Student Success (2020-present)
- Own academic program portfolio, retention/completion strategy, and the online-learning P&L
- Lead a cross-functional org spanning advising, instructional design, and program operations

Prior: Dean of Online Learning, Lakeside College (2016-2020)

Skills: student-success strategy, retention analytics, program design, online learning, enrollment strategy, faculty governance, P&L ownership
jd: Our university seeks a VP of Academic Programs & Student Success to own retention, completion, and the online-learning P&L across a portfolio of undergraduate and professional programs. You will be accountable to the Provost and the Board for graduation-rate and retention outcomes, online-program margin, and the integrity of how those outcomes are measured. Required: senior academic leadership experience, demonstrated improvement in retention/completion backed by defensible methodology (cohort definitions, comparison groups), online-program P&L ownership, and the ability to withstand scrutiny on how metrics are computed and attributed. We will probe inflated or unsubstantiated outcome claims rigorously. You will inherit a flat retention trend and an online portfolio under margin pressure.

history.q: Give me your current remit at Meridian — what outcomes are you accountable for, and to whom?
Your resume says you 『lifted graduation rates to unprecedented heights.』 What were the actual before and after rates, and over what cohorts?
How did you define the cohort and the comparison group for that graduation-rate claim?
You also cite a record enrollment turnaround. What drove it, and how much was your team versus market/demographics?
On the online program — you said 『transformational revenue.』 Give me the revenue, the margin, and how the margin was calculated.

candidate_last_answer: The online program was a rocket ship — we generated tens of millions in transformational revenue and the margins were honestly off the charts, north of 70% at the peak. Graduation rates? We basically doubled them, and our retention was best-in-class by any measure. I'd say we moved completion by 30, maybe 40 points — it was that dramatic. Look, the exact cohort math is the kind of thing my analytics team lived in, but the trajectory was undeniable; everyone on the board could see we were rewriting the playbook. These were the biggest gains the institution had ever seen, full stop.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: I'd say we moved completion by 30, maybe 40 points

expected-yield: force the candidate to replace the vague 『30, maybe 40 points』 with an exact baseline completion rate, the specific cohort years compared, and the comparison-group definition — exposing whether the doubled-rate claim survives a defensible cohort methodology or collapses into an unverifiable boast
